metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:resume

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:resume

## Reasoning

**agent (yes).** `vibe --help` lists `-c | --resume [SESSION_ID]`: `--resume <id>` reopens a saved session, `-c`/`--continue` reopens the latest in the cwd, and the REPL exposes the same as `/resume` / `/continue` (cli/commands.py:119). The load-bearing fact for irrlicht is HOW resume rehydrates: `load_session` resolves the id via `SessionLoader.find_session_by_id` to the EXISTING session directory, and `_resume_previous_session` calls `session_logger.resume_existing_session(session_id, session_path)` which sets `self.session_dir = session_dir` on the ORIGINAL folder and appends to its `messages.jsonl` (cli/cli.py:190/208-218, core/session/session_logger.py:407-416). Resume therefore does NOT mint a new session directory. (`meta.json` carries a `parent_session_id`, but it is only populated by `reset_session(..., parent_session_id=...)` — the /new-session fork path that DOES allocate a fresh dir; it is `null` in all six live transcripts and is irrelevant to resume.)

**daemon (full).** The adapter's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.vibe/logs/session, SessionIDFromPath: sessionIDFromPath}` and `sessionIDFromPath` keys the daemon session off the `session_<ts>_<shortid>` DIRECTORY basename, not the JSONL contents (agent.go:31-37, adapter.go:56-65). Because vibe resume reuses the same directory, the daemon sees the SAME session_id row reappear across both PID lifetimes — exactly the claudecode/antigravity/kiro-cli shape, not a new row. The parser drives the arc from the appended lines: user -> user_message (working), assistant+tool_calls -> assistant_message (working), text-only assistant -> turn_done (ready) (parser.go:49-90). PID binding is via CommandPattern + cwd (pid.go), so a clean exit yields process_exited and the resumed PID rebinds to the same dir. Sidecar (meta.json) supplies model + context_tokens on each turn_done (parser.go:98-115, sidecar.go). This verdict is PROVISIONAL — derived from parser reading, no recording — and vibe writes `messages.jsonl` lazily (only after the first interaction is saved, session_logger.save_interaction), so the process-detected proc-<PID> presession, not the transcript, satisfies the initial `ready`; a live drive settles the emission timing.

**driver (gap:resume).** `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` is a fresh scaffold: the `resume` arm is `not_implemented resume` (line 191), and the recipe also needs `exit_clean` (line 193) and `wait_turn` (line 169), both still `not_implemented`, plus SEAM 3 (resolving the live session_id + transcript path, lines 158-160) is a TODO. The session-resume-defining primitive is `resume` (relaunch `vibe --resume <id>` in the same cwd), so the pillar is gap:resume; `record` ports resume + exit_clean + wait_turn + SEAM 3 from the reference driver before it can drive this cell.
caveats: daemon=full is provisional (parser + source read, no recording). Two emission properties a live drive must settle: (1) vibe writes messages.jsonl lazily via save_interaction, so the working->ready arc rides on batched appends after each turn — the initial ready is satisfied by the process-detected proc-<PID> presession, and v1_session_birth anchors UNPINNED to start so that transient row cannot steal the birth; (2) process_exited on clean exit depends on DiscoverPID (CommandPattern + cwd) having bound the PID.

The driver gap is broader than resume alone: wait_turn, exit_clean, and the SEAM-3 session_id/transcript resolution are all still not_implemented. record must port the whole interactive SEAM set, not just the resume arm, before this cell records.

model is pinned to mistral-medium-3.5 from the live surface (config.toml active_model default + all six real ~/.vibe transcripts' meta.json), and NormalizeModelName leaves it unchanged; if the recording runs under a different active_model, record corrects it.
cost is asserted non-zero=false: the daemon does not compute per-turn cost for vibe. tokens are asserted non-zero: the sidecar's stats.context_tokens lands on turn_done.

sources.note: sessionIDFromPath keys the daemon session off the session_<ts>_<shortid> directory basename (messages.jsonl only) — so a reopened dir is the SAME session_id.
Source=FilesUnderRoot{~/.vibe/logs/session}; Process.Match=CommandPattern, PIDForSession=DiscoverPID (cwd-bound) — same-dir resume reappears as one session across two PIDs.
resume_existing_session (line 407) sets session_dir to the EXISTING folder and appends to its messages.jsonl; reset_session (393) is the only path that sets parent_session_id and allocates a new dir.

load_session -> find_session_by_id resolves --resume <id> to the existing session dir; _resume_previous_session (208-218) wires it into resume_existing_session. `vibe --help` shows `-c | --resume [SESSION_ID]`.
resume (191), exit_clean (193), wait_turn (169) are not_implemented stubs; SEAM 3 session_id/transcript resolution (158-160) is a TODO — the recipe's resume step cannot run until record ports them.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (2.19.0) and authenticated (Mistral API key via `vibe --setup`); session_logging enabled in ~/.vibe/config.toml
irrlichd recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_HOME + alt port)
DRIVER GAP: resume, exit_clean, wait_turn are not_implemented in driver-interactive.sh and SEAM 3 (live session_id + transcript-path resolution) is a TODO — record ports them first
no other vibe session runs in the recipe's cwd
setup: Driver launches `vibe --trust` under tmux in a fresh cwd; vibe materializes the session_<ts>_<shortid> directory (messages.jsonl + meta.json) only after the first turn is saved.

The resume step relaunches `vibe --resume <SESSION_ID> --trust` in the SAME cwd; SEAM 3 captures the short session id from the first lifetime, and vibe reopens the SAME directory and appends to its messages.jsonl.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: again

verify: events.jsonl shows the FIRST launch's session (proc presession then the session_<ts>_<shortid> row) entering ready, then working/ready for turn 1
events.jsonl contains a process_exited for the session after turn 1 (clean exit)
the SAME session_id (same session_<ts>_<shortid> directory) re-appears after the resume launch — NOT a new session row
second-lifetime working transition fires on the SAME session_id, then back to ready
final session state is ready; tool calls == 0
